questions.question: What is Positioning & Stacking and why is it important?
questions.answer: Positioning & Stacking is a fundamental concept that Static, relative, absolute, fixed, sticky positioning and z-index. It's important because it forms the foundation for building robust and efficient applications. Understanding Positioning & Stacking allows developers to make better architectural decisions and write cleaner code.

questions.question: How do you apply Positioning & Stacking in a real project?
questions.answer: In practice, Positioning & Stacking is applied by following established patterns and best practices. Start with the simplest implementation, test thoroughly, and iterate. The key is to understand the underlying principles so you can adapt to different scenarios.

questions.question: What are common mistakes when working with Positioning & Stacking?
questions.answer: Common mistakes include: not understanding the fundamentals before applying advanced patterns, ignoring edge cases, not testing adequately, and over-engineering solutions. The best approach is to start simple and add complexity only when needed.

questions.question: How would you explain Positioning & Stacking to a beginner?
questions.answer: Positioning & Stacking is essentially about static, relative, absolute, fixed, sticky positioning and z-index. Think of it as a building block — once you understand this concept, you can combine it with other concepts to build complex applications.
